hero.title:
The AI coding assistant
that doesn't need Node,
npm, or your patience.
hero.subtitle: Heavily inspired by Claude Code and CodeBuff, BujiCoder is a Go-native multi-agent AI coding tool that runs in your terminal. One binary. Zero dependencies. Pick any LLM provider. Your code never leaves your machine.

features.label: What makes it different
features.title: 10 reasons BujiCoder isn't just another code assistant
features.desc: Most AI coding tools lock you into one model, one provider, and a mountain of JavaScript dependencies. BujiCoder takes a fundamentally different approach.

items.title: Zero-Dependency Go Binary
items.desc: No Node. No npm. No Python. No Docker. A single compiled binary that starts instantly. Download it, run it, done. Under 30 MB for the entire tool.

items.title: Granular Model Control
items.desc: Assign different models to different agent roles. Use a cheap model for file exploration, a powerful one for editing, and a reasoning model for planning — all in a single session.

items.title: Any Provider, Your Choice
items.desc: OpenRouter, Groq, Cerebras, Together AI, Amazon Bedrock, Google Vertex, OpenAI, Anthropic, xAI, Ollama — bring your own API keys and switch providers freely.

items.title: 13 Specialized Agents
items.desc: Not one generic chatbot. A team of 13 purpose-built agents — editor, planner, researcher, reviewer, git committer, and more — each with their own tools and system prompts.

items.title: Parallel Evolution Mode
items.desc: In Max mode, multiple agents implement competing solutions simultaneously. A judge agent evaluates and picks the best one. It's like having a coding tournament for every task.

items.title: Three Cost Modes
items.desc: Normal for fast/cheap everyday coding. Heavy for harder tasks with smarter models. Max for mission-critical work with the most powerful models and parallel evolution.

items.title: Local-First Privacy
items.desc: All tools execute on your machine. File reads, writes, code search, terminal commands — nothing routes through a third-party server. Your code stays yours.

items.title: MCP Extensibility
items.desc: Extend BujiCoder with any Model Context Protocol server. Add GitHub, browser automation, databases, or custom tools. Eager or lazy loading — you control when servers start.

items.title: Built-In Safety Rails
items.desc: 4-level threat classification with 40+ security patterns. Meta-execution detection blocks shell injection via eval, $(), and bash -c. Per-project permissions, three modes — strict, ask, or yolo. Critical threats are always blocked.

items.title: Vision & Multimodal
items.desc: Attach images directly with @path/to/screenshot.png. Review UI mockups, debug visual regressions, or let the UI reviewer agent automate browser testing with Puppeteer.

providers.label: LLM Providers
providers.title: Your keys. Your models. Your money.
providers.desc: BujiCoder talks directly to AI providers using your API keys. No middleman markup. No vendor lock-in. Switch providers with a single config change.

howItWorks.label: How it works
howItWorks.title: From install to coding in 60 seconds

steps.title: Install
steps.desc: One curl command. One binary. No runtime dependencies.

steps.title: Configure
steps.desc: The setup wizard walks you through picking a provider and entering your API key. Quick setup takes 10 seconds.

steps.title: Code
steps.desc: Ask questions, edit files, search your codebase, run commands. BujiCoder's agents handle the rest.

inspired.label: Standing on the shoulders of giants
inspired.title: Inspired by the best in the game
inspired.desc: BujiCoder didn't emerge in a vacuum. We studied the tools that redefined AI-assisted coding, learned what they got right, and built on those ideas with our own vision.

cards.name: Claude Code
cards.desc: Claude Code proved that a terminal-first AI assistant could be more powerful than any IDE plugin. Its agenticarchitecture — where the AI reads files, edits code, runs commands, and iterates on its own — fundamentally changed how developers interact with AI. BujiCoder's multi-agent orchestration, tool-use system, and permission model are all deeply influenced by the patterns Claude Code pioneered.

cards.name: CodeBuff
cards.desc: CodeBuff showed that an AI coding tool doesn't need to be a bloated IDE extension with a hundred config files. It championed speed, simplicity, and getting out of the developer's way. BujiCoder carries that same philosophy forward — a single binary, instant startup, minimal config — while adding the flexibility to bring your own models and providers.

inspired.cta:
We took the best ideas, rewrote them in Go, made them provider-agnostic, and added multi-agent superpowers.
That's BujiCoder.

install.label: Install
install.title: Ready to try it?
install.desc: Pick your platform. Be coding in under a minute.

cards.title: Windows
cards.desc: Download the latest .exe from GitHub Releases.

footer.tagline: AI coding assistant. Your models. Your machine. Your rules.
